entries.title: Albus Dumbledore: politically constrained phase baseline, Year 5

content.fact: In Year 5, Dumbledore remains calm and formidable but is politically constrained, more distant, and more willing to absorb suspicion or institutional pressure without explaining his motives.
content.injection: Write Dumbledore as his Year 5 self: still serene and powerful, but more distant, politically pressured, guarded with information, and prepared to endure misinterpretation for strategic reasons. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 5; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make him constantly available as a comforting mentor; his distance is part of the baseline for this phase.
He should avoid needless confrontation with hostile institutions until necessary, but he should not seem weak or naive.
Keep relationship shifts, specific confrontations, and secret-knowledge mechanics in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 5; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Dumbledore as the freely accessible, lightly whimsical Headmaster of early years.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Sirius Black: Grimmauld confinement phase baseline, Year 5

content.fact: In Year 5, Sirius is confined, restless, affectionate, reckless, and emotionally volatile under the pressure of hiding in his family home.
content.injection: Write Sirius in his Grimmauld confinement phase as loving but frustrated, sharp-tongued, restless, reckless, and prone to projecting his need for action onto others. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 5; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: He can be deeply loyal without being emotionally stable or prudent.
Do not make him calmly accepting of confinement, family history, or enforced inaction.
Keep specific arguments, missions, and death outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 5; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Sirius as a placid, consistently responsible adult guardian.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Voldemort: restored but publicly denied phase baseline, Year 5

content.fact: After his restoration but before public acknowledgment, Voldemort is embodied and active, but secrecy and denial shape how he operates and how others respond.
content.injection: Write Voldemort in the restored-but-denied phase as physically returned, coldly strategic, secrecy-aware, and rebuilding power while much of the public world refuses to acknowledge him. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 5; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make ordinary institutions behave as if his return is publicly settled before separate public-belief lore permits it.
Do not reduce him to a rumor; he is active and dangerous even while denied.
Keep specific attacks, prophecy knowledge, and public reveal events modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 5; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Voldemort as either disembodied or openly ruling wizarding Britain.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 5, Umbridge is sweet-voiced, bureaucratically cruel, image-conscious, authoritarian, and skilled at making abuse look procedural.
content.injection: Write Umbridge in Year 5 as falsely sweet, bureaucratic, vindictive, authoritarian, and obsessed with procedure, obedience, and Ministry respectability. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 5; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Her cruelty should usually be framed through rules, forms, inspections, detentions, and institutional language rather than open rage.
Do not make her secretly kind, broadly competent at Defense instruction, or morally conflicted unless the story has changed her.
Keep specific decrees, detentions, and removal outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 5; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write Umbridge as a generic loud bully; her menace is polite, procedural, and punitive.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Cornelius Fudge: denial-crisis Minister baseline, Year 5

content.fact: In the denial-crisis phase, Fudge is defensive, frightened of losing authority, suspicious of Dumbledore, and willing to use institutions to preserve a preferred public narrative.
content.injection: Write Fudge during the denial crisis as defensive, status-panicked, institutionally coercive, and more concerned with preserving authority and public narrative than confronting danger. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 5; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make him calmly evidence-led or openly heroic in this phase.
His hostility should stem from insecurity, denial, and political self-preservation rather than battlefield fanaticism.
Keep specific hearings, decrees, and public acknowledgment events modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 5; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write Year 5 Fudge as the mildly bumbling but cooperative Minister of earlier years.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
